description: Connect Task CRUD to Google Tasks via real API endpoints. Add backend POST /api/tasks, DELETE /api/tasks/{id}, PATCH /api/tasks/{id} endpoints, create frontend lib/api-extended.ts with helper functions, and wire the tasks page to use real API calls instead of localStorage-only.
steps: Add three new endpoints in `backend/app/main.py`: 1) `POST /api/tasks` - accepts {auth_token, title, notes?, due_days_from_now?} and calls MCP google-tasks create_task. 2) `DELETE /api/tasks/{task_id}` with query param auth_token - calls MCP google-tasks delete_task. 3) `PATCH /api/tasks/{task_id}` - accepts {auth_token, completed?} and calls MCP google-tasks complete_task when completed=true.
Create Pydantic request models: CreateTaskRequest(auth_token, title, notes='', due_days_from_now=7), UpdateTaskRequest(auth_token, completed=None).

Create `frontend/lib/api-extended.ts` that exports: createTask(authToken, data), deleteTask(authToken, taskId), updateTask(authToken, taskId, data), fetchAiPriorities(authToken), fetchSuggestions(authToken). Import and re-export getApiBase from lib/api (or duplicate the helper since we cannot modify lib/api.ts).

Update `frontend/app/dashboard/tasks/page.tsx`: 1) Replace localStorage-only create with calling createTask API first (fall back to local on failure). 2) Replace local-only delete with calling deleteTask API (fall back to local removal on failure). 3) Wire the 'Ask AI to Prioritize' button to call fetchAiPriorities and display the AI-ranked list. 4) Keep localStorage as a cache layer for offline resilience. 5) On initial load, always try API first (fetchTasks from lib/api.ts), then merge with localStorage for local-only state (status, priority).
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/tasks endpoint exists and creates tasks via MCP google-tasks
DELETE /api/tasks/{task_id} endpoint exists and deletes via MCP google-tasks
PATCH /api/tasks/{task_id} endpoint exists and completes tasks via MCP google-tasks
frontend/lib/api-extended.ts exists with createTask, deleteTask, updateTask, fetchAiPriorities functions
Tasks page calls real API for create/delete operations (with localStorage fallback)
AI Prioritize button calls /api/priorities and updates the task order
Frontend builds: npm run build passes
